docs.title: MC-45 SPOT DEPOSITOR
docs.text: Version 1.0 / Model MC-45 LH and RH / Date: June 2026 / Serial Number: [_______]

docs.title: 1.1 Emergency Stop Devices
docs.text:
Three emergency stop devices are installed on the Depositor: one on the OI enclosure, one on the ELECTRICAL ENCLOSURE, and one on the FEED-Z CONVEYOR (option). When pressed, the device removes all motion from the Depositor. To reset, pull the actuator to release.

Figure 1.1a Emergency Stop Locations

Figure 1.1b Emergency Stop Locations

For the status of all emergency stop devices and guard switches, navigate to the Safety PLC screen on the OI. See Section 12: OI Reference.

docs.title: 1.2 Guard Switches
docs.text:
Seven safety guard switches are monitored by the safety PLC. The Depositor will not run if any guard is open or removed.

Switch Location ENTRY Entry guard MIDDLE Middle guard EXIT Exit guard PORTION FRONT Portion belt assembly PORTION BACK Portion belt assembly SPINNER Spinner area HOPPER Hopper area

Figure 1.2a Guard Switch Locations

Figure 1.2b Guard Switch Locations

docs.title: 1.3 Safe State Definition
docs.text:
Pressing an emergency stop device or opening any guard switch places the Depositor in a safe state. The following occur automatically:

  • All motion commands are removed. Motors coast to stop.
  • The RETRACTABLE CONVEYOR servo drive is disabled.
  • Pneumatic air supply to the BUCKET cylinder is removed.

The MACHINE ENABLE circuit must be reset before operation can resume. See Section 3: Startup.

Note

Emergency stop devices are for emergency conditions only. For normal shutdown, use the STOP control on the HOME screen.

docs.title: 2.1 Component Identification
docs.text:
The diagrams below identify all major components.

Figure 2.1a Primary components, callouts [1]–[11] # Component 1 PRODUCT CONVEYOR 2 PRODUCT CONVEYOR ENCODER 3 PORTION CONVEYOR 4 SPINNER MOTOR 5 RAKE MOTOR 6 BUCKET 7 CARPET 8 PORTION CONVEYOR MOTOR 9 TARGET SENSOR 10 EXIT GUARD 11 OPERATOR INTERFACE

Figure 2.1b Primary components, callouts [12]–[23] # Component 12 MIDDLE GUARD 13 ENTRY GUARD 14 PORTION GUARD (FRONT) 15 PORTION GUARD (BACK) 16 AIR SUPPLY 17 HOPPER 18 CONTROL ENCLOSURE 19 FEED-Z CONTROL ENCLOSURE (Option) 20 FEED-Z GUARD (Option) 21 FEED-Z CONVEYOR MOTOR (Option) 22 RAKE GUARD 23 RAKE HEIGHT INDICATOR

Note

Component descriptions are in Appendix B: Glossary. Control panel layout is in Section 2.2: Control Panel Layout.

docs.title: 2.2 Control Panel Layout
docs.text: Figure 2.2  Control Enclosure Layout # Component 1 ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT 2 CIRCUIT BREAKERS 3 VARIABLE FREQUENCY DRIVES (VFD) 4 SERVO DRIVES 5 SAFETY CONTACTORS AND SAFETY PLC 6 ANALOG SIGNAL CONDITIONER 7 POWER SUPPLY 8 NETWORK SWITCHES 9 WIRE TERMINALS 10 PLC AND I/O 11 TRANSFORMER

docs.title: 3.1 Startup Sequence
docs.text:
Figure 3.1 Startup sequence flowchart

  1. Confirm the Depositor is fully reassembled. Belts, guards, and scrapers are all in place.

  2. Verify the compressed air supply is ON.

  3. Turn the ELECTRICAL DISCONNECT to the ON position. Wait for the PLC and OI to finish initializing. The HOME screen appears when ready. If active alarms are present, the OI displays the ALARMS screen first. Navigate to the ALARMS screen, correct all conditions, then press FAULT RESET once to clear all resettable faults.

    Note

    If the MACHINE ENABLE indicator flashes: pull all E-STOP actuators to release, close all guards, then press MACHINE ENABLE again. If it continues to flash, navigate to MANUAL → SAFETY PLC to identify which device remains open. Faults that do not clear after pressing FAULT RESET require device-specific corrective action. Navigate to the ALARMS screen and refer to Section 13: Troubleshooting for guidance.

  4. Press the MACHINE ENABLE PUSH BUTTON. Verify the MACHINE ENABLE indicator is illuminated solid.

  5. When all criteria are satisfied, proceed to Section 4: Operating Modes.

docs.title: 4  Operating Modes
docs.text: Sections 4.3 through 4.6 describe the four operator-selected modes. PRIME and CONSERVE are automatic states. The Depositor enters PRIME and CONSERVE modes based on operating conditions.

docs.title: 4.1 Depositor Control: LOCAL and REMOTE
docs.text:
Figure 4.1 LOCAL and REMOTE control flowchart
  1. LOCAL control: Start and stop the Depositor from the HOME screen using PRESS TO START and STOP. The Depositor runs independently of any upstream or downstream equipment.
  2. REMOTE control: An external line signal starts and stops the Depositor. A dry-contact output (Upstream Interlock) sends the Depositor's run status to upstream equipment.

docs.title: 4.2 Mode Selection
docs.text:
Figure 4.2 Operating mode selection flowchart
  1. Run Production. Select RUN mode for normal topping application. The Depositor verifies priming criteria before starting. If not met, the OI prompts the operator to complete PRIME mode first.
  2. Empty Depositor. Select EMPTY mode to clear topping from the Depositor.
  3. Bypass Depositor (Pass-Thru). Select BYPASS mode to run the PRODUCT CONVEYOR without applying topping.

docs.title: 4.3 PRIME / RUN Mode
docs.text: Figure 4.3  PRIME and RUN mode flowchart

docs.title: PRIME Mode
docs.text:
1. Select PRIME mode. Press and hold PRESS TO START for three seconds.
  2. The RAKE moves to the recipe position. Allow travel to complete before loading topping.
  3. Load topping manually at the HOPPER.
  4. Priming continues until PORTION CONVEYOR weight exceeds 75% of TARGET LEVEL for 10 continuous seconds.
  5. When complete, the Depositor automatically transitions to RUN mode.

Note

PRIME mode is available only in LOCAL control. Selecting RUN mode before priming is complete prompts the OI to require PRIME mode to finish first.

docs.title: RUN Mode
docs.text:
When priming is complete, the Depositor transitions to RUN mode automatically. The PRODUCT CONVEYOR runs continuously. The RAKE, SPINNER, PORTION CONVEYOR, and FEED-Z CONVEYOR start when a target is detected.

The STATUS BANNER displays MACHINE RUNNING.

docs.title: 4.4 EMPTY Mode
docs.text:
Figure 4.4 EMPTY mode flowchart
  1. Select EMPTY mode. Press and hold PRESS TO START for three seconds. All conveyor drives run to clear topping from the system. Watch the HOPPER and RAKE area. When topping no longer recirculates and the belt surfaces are clear, press STOP.

Note

EMPTY mode does not stop automatically. Press STOP when emptying is complete. EMPTY mode is available in LOCAL control only.

docs.title: 4.5 BYPASS Mode
docs.text:
Figure 4.5 BYPASS mode flowchart
  1. Select BYPASS mode. Start the Depositor. The PRODUCT CONVEYOR operates without topping application.

Note

When equipped with a safety PLC, BYPASS mode allows guards to remain open. Contact Grote Service before running BYPASS with a guard open if the installed safety circuit type is not known.

docs.title: 4.6 CONSERVE Mode
docs.text:
Figure 4.6 CONSERVE mode flowchart
  1. During RUN mode, the Depositor monitors the TARGET SENSOR.
  2. If no target is detected, the CONSERVE timer starts. A target arriving before the timer expires resets the timer and operation continues normally.
  3. If the timer expires, the Depositor enters CONSERVE mode.
  4. All operations except the PRODUCT CONVEYOR pause: the RAKE, SPINNER, PORTION CONVEYOR, and FEED-Z CONVEYOR stop.
  5. The STATUS BANNER displays MACHINE RUNNING CONSERVE MODE ACTIVE.
  6. When a target is detected, CONSERVE mode clears automatically and all stopped motors restart.

Note

Set the CONSERVE timer at MACHINE OPTIONS → CONSERVE MODE TIME [ms], in milliseconds. The value should be based on travel time from the TARGET SENSOR to the drop point at the expected line speed.
